description.p: A mixture of arcane prowess and precise bladework, the Duskblades are a rare and powerful group who use ancient teachings from bygone ages to empower themselves in the modern day. Most Duskblades live in small monasteries, hidden deep within the mountains or deserts. Any Duskblade Orders that do exist within wider society has to contend with abuse of the arcane from those who would wish to use the power of their group for their own ends. Some Duskblades even work with wizard academies for protection, sharing their knowledge in exchange for a safe sanctuary.

The combination of arcane arts and bladework is tricky to learn and almost impossible to master. Those who take the long route of learning these techniques often dedicate themselves fully to their studies, ensuring that they are prepared to mix spells with sword in the most effective way possible.

tr.td: Choose 2: Arcana, Athletics, History, Insight, Intimidation, Medicine, Religion, or Survival
Light and Medium Armour and Shields
Choose A, B: (A) Longsword, Javelin, Chain Mail, Shield, and an Arcane Focus or (B) 125 GP

ul.li: Gain all the traits in the Core Duskblade Traits table.
Gain the Duskblade's level 1 features, which are listed in the Duskblade Features table.
Gain the following traits from the Core Duskblade Traits table: Hit Point Die, proficiency with Martial weapons, and training with Light and Medium armor and Shields.
Gain the Duskblade's level 1 features, which are listed in the Duskblade Features table.

tr.td: Cantrips/td> Spell Slots per Spell Level
Fighting Style, Arcane Empowerment, Arcane Strike

description.p: As a Duskblade, you gain the following class features when you reach the specified Duskblade levels. These features are listed in the Duskblade Features table.

setters.set: An Arcane Fighter of Mystical Arts
Martial weapons, Light armor, Medium armor, Shields

description.p: You have learned to channel the magical essence of nature to cast spells.
Spell Slots. The Duskblade Features table shows how many spell slots you have to cast your level 1+ spells. You regain all expended slots when you finish a Long Rest.
Prepared Spells of Level 1+. You prepare the list of level 1+ spells that are available for you to cast with this feature. To start, choose two level 1 Duskblade spells.

The number of spells on your list increases as you gain Duskblade levels, as shown in the Prepared Spells column of the Duskblade Features table. Whenever that number increases, choose additional Duskblade spells until the number of spells on your list matches the number in the Duskblade Features table. The chosen spells must be of a level for which you have spell slots. For example, if you're a level 5 Duskblade, your list of prepared spells can include six Duskblade spells of level 1 or 2 in any combination.

If another Duskblade feature gives you spells that you always have prepared, those spells don't count against the number of spells you can prepare with this feature, but those spells otherwise count as Duskblade spells for you.
Changing Your Prepared Spells. Whenever you finish a Long Rest, you can replace one spell on your list with another Duskblade spell for which you have spell slots.
Spellcasting Ability. Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for your Duskblade spells.
Spellcasting Focus. You can use an Arcane Focus as a Spellcasting Focus for your Duskblade spells.

center.p: Spell save DC = 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Intelligence modifier
Spell attack modifier = your proficiency bonus + your Intelligence modifier

sheet.description: Whenever you finish a Long Rest, you can replace one spell on your list with another Duskblade spell.

description.p: Your training with weapons allows you to use the mastery properties of two kinds of Simple or Martial weapons of your choice. Whenever you finish a Long Rest, you can practice weapon drills and change one of those weapon choices.

sheet.description: Whenever you finish a Long Rest, you can change one of the weapons you previously chosen for a different one.

description.p: Starting at 2nd level, your training as a Duskblade has allowed you to learn a series of special techniques that can be used to protect your allies and improve your offensive capabilities. As a bonus action, you activate this ward for the next minute or until you are Incapacitated. While this ward is active, you and allies within 10 feet of you cannot be Frightened. In addition, damage dealt to allies within 10 feet of you is halved.
You can use this feature twice. You regain one expended use at the end of a Short Rest and all expended uses at the end of a Long Rest.

At 9th level, the range of the aura is increased to 20 feet and you gain an additional usage of this feature. At 18th level, the range of the aura is increased to 30 feet and you gain an additional usage of this feature.

sheet.description: You activate this ward for the next minute or until you are Incapacitated. While this ward is active, you and allies within {{duskblade ward:range}} feet of you cannot be Frightened. In addition, damage dealt to allies within {{duskblade ward:range}} feet of you is halved.

description.p: You have honed your martial prowess and gain a Fighting Style feat of your choice. Defense is recommended.
Whenever you gain a Duskblade level, you can replace the feat you chose with a different Fighting Style feat.

You gain a Duskblade subclass of your choice. A subclass is a specialization that grants you features at certain Duskblade levels. For the rest of your career, you gain each of your subclass's features that are of your Duskblade level or lower.
You gain the Ability Score Improvement feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. You gain this feature again at Duskblade levels 8, 10, 12 and 16.

By 5th level, you have learnt how to inspire courage into your allies during dire times. As an action, you make a loud battle cry to all allied creatures within 60 feet that can hear you. Creatures that hear your battle cry gain temporary hit points equal to 1d12 plus your duskblade level and gain a Heroic Inspiration if they lack it.
Once you have used this feature, you cannot use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

sheet.description: You make a loud battle cry to all allied creatures within 60 feet that can hear you. Creatures that hear your battle cry gain temporary hit points equal to 1d12+{{rallying:cry}} and gain a Heroic Inspiration if they lack it.

description.p: Starting at 5th level, your Duskblade training allows you to strike a decisive blow against your foes. When you make an attack with a melee weapon that you are proficient with, you can add 2d8 damage of your weapon’s damage type to the damage rolls. You can only add this extra damage once per turn.
The damage is increased to 3d8 at 9th level, 4d8 at 13th level and 5d8 at 17th level.

sheet.description: When you make an attack with a melee weapon that you are proficient with, you can add {{weakpoint:damage}}d8 damage of your weapon’s damage type to the damage rolls. You can only add this extra damage once per turn.

description.p: When you activate your Duskblade Ward, you and all allies within its range gain a +2 bonus to their AC. In addition, once per round when an ally fails a saving throw while within 10 feet of you, you can make them reroll. They must use the new result.

sheet.description: When you activate your Duskblade Ward, you and all allies within its range gain a +2 bonus to their AC. In addition, once per round when an ally fails a saving throw while within 10 feet of you, you can make them reroll. They must use the new result.

description.p: Choose two saving throws. You gain proficiency if you do not already have it. If you already have proficiency in the saving throw you choose, you instead double your proficiency bonus for those saving throws.

You put your heart into each and every strike. You can add a bonus to damage rolls made with weapons with which you are proficient equal to your Constitution modifier. In addition, when you make an attack of opportunity, you can take two attacks instead of one.

sheet.description: You can add {{GREATER_MYSTICAL_KNOWLEDGE:mod}} extra damage to rolls made with weapons with which you are proficient with. In addition, when you make an attack of opportunity, you can take two attacks instead of one.

description.p: You are unstoppable while in combat. You reduce all damage you take by an amount equal to your Constitution modifier. In addition, any damage you deal with a weapon that you are proficient with deals additional damage equal to your Constitution modifier.

sheet.description: You reduce all damage you take by {{GREATER_MYSTICAL_KNOWLEDGE:mod}}. In addition, any damage you deal with a weapon that you are proficient with deals an additional {{GREATER_MYSTICAL_KNOWLEDGE:mod}} damage.

description.p: You can dig deep when times grow tough. While you are under half of your maximum hp, all attacks you make with weapons that you are proficient with are made at advantage.

sheet.description: While you are under half of your maximum hp, all attacks you make with weapons that you are proficient with are made at advantage.

description.p: You gain an Epic Boon feat or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

Upon reaching 20th level, you have become a paragon for defending and protecting your allies and you are able to resist even the forces of death for a time. As a bonus action, you can choose to prepare yourself against attack. For the next minute, you gain resistance to all incoming damage that you take. In addition, if you are reduced to 0 hit points while this is active, you are instead on 1 hit point and conscious. Once you use this feature, you must complete a long rest before you use it again.

sheet.description: You can choose to prepare yourself against attack. For the next minute, you gain resistance to all incoming damage that you take. In addition, if you are reduced to 0 hit points while this is active, you are instead on 1 hit point and conscious.
